title: Keppel and Aster Partner for Major Ethanol-to-Jet SAF Facility in Singapore
content: Keppel Ltd. and Aster have entered an agreement to jointly develop one of Asiaâ€™s first commercial-scale Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) facilities on Jurong Island, Singapore. The proposed plant is designed to produce up to 100,000 tonnes of SAF annually by converting low-carbon ethanol into aviation fuel. Following a successful feasibility study, the partners will now conduct Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) studies to finalize technical and commercial details. This project aligns with Singaporeâ€™s national targets to become a premier SAF hub and decarbonize the aviation sector. Supported by the Economic Development Board and Enterprise Singapore, the facility will leverage Jurong Islandâ€™s industrial infrastructure to supply cleaner fuel to regional airlines, addressing the growing demand for scalable decarbonization solutions in the Asia-Pacific market.
author: Analysts Account
